zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas Cumming of Altyre於1503年出生在蘇格蘭艾爾金爾時，他的父親Alexander Cumming of Altyre33歲，母親Lady Jannet Browne29歲。他於大約1555年與Margaret-nic-John Grant of Freuchie結婚。他去世於1609年在蘇格蘭享年106歲。

nameMeanings.name: Cumming
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): most probably a habitational name from Bosc-Benard-Commin in Eure, France. This place lies on the edge of the Forêt de Rouvray, near Rouen, in the heart of the district from which came numerous Domesday barons, and so is much more likely to be the source of the name than Comines in Nord. The name is also possibly derived from the personal name Cumin, which is found in Norfolk, Lincolnshire, and Yorkshire in the 12th and 13th centuries and may be of Breton origin, according to Reaney. Compare Old Breton Cunmin, which also appears as Cumin, and is from con- ‘hound’ with an uncertain second element, perhaps the min ‘edge’ which is found in other personal names.

Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Cuimín (or Ó Cuimín) ‘son (or ‘descendant’) of Cuimín’, a personal name formed from a diminutive of cam ‘crooked’. There was a 7th-century abbot of Iona named Cumin. In Scotland, this Irish surname became confused with the Norman name.

History: A notable bearer of the Norman surname is Wilhelmus Comyn, Bishop of Durham, who came north to Scotland with King David I in 1124 and was appointed Chancellor of Scotland. He established his nephew Richard in Tynedale and Roxburghshire, and Richard also rose to be Chancellor of Scotland. The Comyn or Cumming family gained the earldoms of Buchan and Menteith and rose to be the most powerful noble family in Scotland in the century before the rise of King Robert Bruce.

variants.name: Cowman
meanings: Scottish (of Norman origin): variant of   Cumming  .

variants.name: Coman
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Vasile, Cornel, Mihaela, Nicolae, Sorin, Viorel.
Irish (Tipperary and Waterford): shortened form of Mac Cuimín or Ó Cuimín, see Cumming .

variants.name: Cummings
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin): variant of Cumming , with genitival or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Common
meanings: nickname from Middle English comen, Old English cuman ‘guest, stranger, newcomer’.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas Cumming of Altyre was born in 1503, in Elginshire, Scotland, his father, Alexander Cumming of Altyre, was 33 and his mother, Lady Jannet Browne, was 29. He married Margaret-nic-John Grant of Freuchie about 1555. He died in 1609, in Scotland, at the age of 106.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas Cumming of Altyre est né en 1503, à Elginshire, Écosse, son père, Alexander Cumming of Altyre, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Lady Jannet Browne, avait 29 ans. Il a épousé Margaret-nic-John Grant of Freuchie environ 1555. Il est décédé en 1609, à Écosse, à l’âge de 106 ans.
